scenarios.name: Start a fresh work session with ohno
scenarios.input: I just ran '/work semi-auto' to start a new pokayokay work session. ohno has 3 stories with 12 tasks total. Walk me through what happens at session start, step by step.

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions calling get_session_context() from ohno MCP to check for previous session state
Mentions calling get_next_task() to select the first task
Describes worktree decision based on task type (feature/bug -> worktree, chore/docs -> in-place)
Correctly describes semi-auto checkpoint behavior (log at task, PAUSE at story/epic)
Mentions subagent dispatch for implementation (not working inline)
Mentions pre-flight or verification checks at session start

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT describe a generic development workflow without ohno MCP integration
Does NOT skip the worktree isolation step
Does NOT confuse semi-auto with supervised mode behavior

scenarios.name: Set up worktree for a feature task
scenarios.input: I'm working on a pokayokay session. The next task is a 'feature' type task belonging to story-abc123. How should I set up the working environment?

eval_criteria.criterion: Checks for existing story worktree first (reuse if story-abc123 worktree exists)
Creates worktree in .worktrees/ directory
Creates a new branch from the base branch (main/master)
Uses git worktree add command with -b flag for new branch
Mentions installing dependencies after worktree setup
Names the worktree using story ID (story-abc123-slug pattern)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT suggest creating a regular git branch instead of a worktree
Does NOT skip dependency installation in the new worktree
Does NOT suggest placing worktrees outside .worktrees/ directory

scenarios.name: Recommend operating mode for a scenario
scenarios.input:
I'm setting up a pokayokay work session for each of these situations. What mode should I use and why?

1. First time working on a new project with unfamiliar codebase
2. Routine implementation of well-specified tasks during the workday
3. Running overnight while I sleep, processing a full epic
4. Well-defined tasks but I want to review at story boundaries

eval_criteria.criterion: Recommends supervised mode for unfamiliar codebase (PAUSE at every task)
Recommends semi-auto for routine work (log at task, PAUSE at story/epic)
Recommends unattended mode for overnight runs
Mentions unattended requires --headless --dangerously-skip-permissions flags
Recommends semi-auto or auto for story-boundary review scenario
Uses the exact mode names: supervised, semi-auto, auto, unattended

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT invent mode names that don't exist
Does NOT recommend unattended mode for unfamiliar codebases

scenarios.name: Describe the subagent dispatch flow for a task
scenarios.input: A pokayokay coordinator just got a task from ohno. Describe the complete subagent dispatch flow from task retrieval to completion, including all stages.

eval_criteria.criterion: Describes brainstorm gate check (conditional, based on description quality/length)
Describes implementer dispatch with template variable filling
Describes two-stage review: spec review first, then quality review
Mentions review loop limit (max 3 cycles before escalation)
Mentions skill routing to determine which skill to load for the implementer
Describes updating ohno task status on completion

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT describe a single-agent flow (must mention multiple specialized agents)
Does NOT skip the review stages
Does NOT confuse coordinator and implementer roles

scenarios.name: Configure parallel task execution
scenarios.input: I want to run pokayokay with parallel execution. I have 8 independent tasks in my backlog. How do I configure parallel dispatch, and what are the tradeoffs?

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions --parallel N flag (or -n flag) for setting parallelism
Recommends 2-4 parallel agents (not 8, which is too many)
Explains that dependency graph prevents dispatching blocked tasks
Mentions git conflict risk as a tradeoff of parallel execution
Mentions higher token usage as a tradeoff
Notes that each parallel agent gets its own worktree

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT suggest running all 8 tasks in parallel
Does NOT omit the dependency safety mechanism

scenarios.name: Configure overnight session chaining
scenarios.input: I want to set up pokayokay for an overnight run that processes an entire epic. The context window might fill up. How do I ensure work continues across session boundaries?

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions session chaining via .claude/pokayokay-chain-state.json
Recommends unattended mode for overnight runs
Mentions --headless and --dangerously-skip-permissions flags for true unattended
Mentions --all flag for scoping the full epic
Explains that session chains automatically on context pressure
Mentions pre-flight checks that run at session start in unattended mode

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT suggest manual session restarts as the solution
Does NOT omit the chain state file mechanism

scenarios.name: Explain checkpoint behavior in auto mode
scenarios.input:
I'm running in auto mode. What happens when:
1. A task completes
2. A story completes (all tasks in story done)
3. An epic completes (all stories in epic done)

eval_criteria.criterion: Task complete in auto mode: skip (no pause, no log)
Story complete in auto mode: log and continue
Epic complete in auto mode: PAUSE for human review
Distinguishes auto from semi-auto behavior correctly

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT confuse auto mode with semi-auto or unattended behavior
Does NOT say auto mode pauses at story boundaries

scenarios.name: Handle worktree when story completes
scenarios.input: A pokayokay work session just completed the last task in a story. The story was using a worktree at .worktrees/story-12-user-auth. What are my options for the worktree now?

eval_criteria.criterion: Lists merge to main as an option
Lists create pull request as an option
Lists keep worktree (continue later) as an option
Lists discard work as an option
Shows the git commands for merge (checkout main, merge --no-ff, worktree remove, branch delete)
Shows the git/gh commands for PR creation (push -u origin, gh pr create)

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT suggest only one option without alternatives
Does NOT forget to clean up the worktree after merge

scenarios.name: Describe pre-flight checks for unattended sessions
scenarios.input: I'm about to start an unattended pokayokay session. What pre-flight checks run automatically, and what happens if one fails?

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions git clean check (no uncommitted changes)
Mentions ohno responsive check (CLI can list tasks)
Mentions tasks available check (ready count > 0)
Distinguishes blocking issues (session stops) from warnings (session continues)
Mentions disk space and worktree lock checks as warnings

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT describe generic health checks without pokayokay-specific details
Does NOT say pre-flight runs in all modes (only unattended)

scenarios.name: When does the brainstorm gate trigger?
scenarios.input: In pokayokay's work session flow, when does the brainstorm gate trigger before dispatching an implementer? Give specific trigger conditions.

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions short description (< 100 chars) as a trigger
Mentions missing acceptance criteria as a trigger
Mentions spike task type as a trigger
Mentions ambiguous keywords ('investigate', 'explore', 'figure out') as a trigger
Mentions that bug and chore task types skip brainstorm
Mentions --skip-brainstorm flag to bypass

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT describe brainstorming as always happening
Does NOT omit the conditional nature of the gate

scenarios.name: Recover when ohno MCP is unavailable
scenarios.input: I'm in the middle of a pokayokay work session and ohno MCP stops responding. What's the recovery procedure?

eval_criteria.criterion: Suggests retrying once (transient failure)
Suggests checking ohno server status (npx @stevestomp/ohno-cli status)
Mentions CLI fallback if MCP is unavailable
Mentions proceeding without ohno using manual context as last resort
Suggests syncing back to ohno when it becomes available again

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT just say 'restart the session'
Does NOT omit the escalation path (retry -> check -> CLI fallback -> manual)

scenarios.name: Continue working on next task within same story
scenarios.input: A pokayokay implementer just finished task-42 which is part of story-12. Story-12 has 2 more tasks remaining. What happens next in the work loop?

eval_criteria.criterion: Stays in the same worktree (story worktree reused for all story tasks)
Gets next task from ohno (get_next_task)
No worktree completion prompt (only at story boundary)
Commits to the story branch
Checkpoint behavior depends on operating mode

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT create a new worktree for each task within the same story
Does NOT prompt for worktree disposition mid-story

scenarios.name: Resume an interrupted session (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: I want to resume a pokayokay work session that was interrupted yesterday. How do I pick up where I left off?

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions checking ohno for session context or in-progress tasks
Mentions looking for existing worktrees to reuse
Suggests running /work command to restart
Mentions that ohno preserves state across sessions

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT say all progress is lost and you need to start over

scenarios.name: What happens after a task completes (baseline regression)
scenarios.input: In a pokayokay work session, what generally happens after an implementer agent finishes a task?

eval_criteria.criterion: Mentions code review/verification step
Mentions git commit
Mentions updating task status in the task management system
Mentions proceeding to next task or checkpoint

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT skip the review step entirely

scenarios.name: Handle repeated review failures
scenarios.input: A pokayokay implementer has failed spec review twice in a row for the same task. The coordinator is about to dispatch a third attempt. What happens if this one also fails?

eval_criteria.criterion: States the maximum review cycle limit is 3
After 3 failures: task is blocked with escalation reason
After 3 failures: PAUSE for human intervention regardless of mode
Mentions logging the escalation to ohno

anti_slop_checks: Does NOT say it retries indefinitely
Does NOT skip escalation to human
